title: Gothofreda fournieriana
intro: Gothofreda fournieriana, also known as Jamaican Dogwood, is a tropical tree native to the Caribbean, particularly in Jamaica, and is a member of the Anacardiaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to reduce anxiety and insomnia due to its sedative properties, as well as its anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects. The bioactive constituents of Gothofreda fournieriana include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Gothofreda fournieriana is often prepared as a tincture or infusion, and is also used in the production of herbal teas and capsules.
taxonomy: Gothofreda fournieriana is a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae. It is native to New Caledonia, a French territory in the Pacific Ocean. The classification of Gothofreda fournieriana includes its kingdom as Plantae, and its classification as a flowering plant further specifies it as an angiosperm. Within the family Malpighiaceae, Gothofreda fournieriana is classified as a genus and species, with the specific epithet fournieriana honoring a person or source. Further classification of the plant includes its order Crossosomatales, subclass Rosidae, and clade core eudicots.
